ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Belaa o binomoa la ŋɔ o nime be ŋɔ, “Name te ya mɛɛ dɛ hwɛɛh, asɛ baala toole harɛ nɔ ta bobɛlbwa dɛ ba mo yaa ya dɔ bii ɡɛ baa dɛ ɛa bobɛlbwa la.
translated: They said to the people of the village, "We are coming to ask, is it true that there is a certain woman who has given birth to a child and the child has no father?"

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: A habɔɔ la, Korowii du daa bodɛɛɡe bobɛl baa yerɔ Eden wee leebɔr, dɛ kpɔ baa waa tao la ka so bela.
translated: In the beginning, God created heaven and earth, and the earth was empty and without form.

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: O ɡelɔ o toli la lawɛ ba bii tɔ sie mɔɔ Lea ne o bii teŋɛ, asɛ Rakɛl ne Josef ka mun ta.
translated: The angel said to the two women, "Don't be afraid, for I know you are looking for Jesus who was crucified, as Rachel and Joseph also mourned.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Jekɔb ŋwɛnɛ waa valɔ la, o kpɔ sie chaɡɛ wee leebɔr dɛ kaa bela harɛ la ta.
translated: Jacob's wife came to the farm, she carried a small basket on her head to go and work on the farm there.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Anomanta, o ta nɛwɛnyina, ɡɛ tenten waa doa laanee o ta ba. Baa ne haahnee o ta.
translated: Anomanta, or the spider, has built a very big house and is living inside. His grandmother is visiting him.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Anomanta, Nowa, ka nyin daa waa kpeɡa, dɛ́ kwaa dakajen waa won nee sie vala kɛŋɔ ɡbera, ásɛ dɛ bɔne o bini kɛŋɔ deene, ásɛ dɛ kpɔ kootal maa o bini ne o habɔɔ bwa.
translated: Anomanta, Nowa, when the sun sets and night falls, and the stars shine brightly in the sky, then the spirits of the dead will come out to dance, then the sound of the drums will beat loudly and the spirits will dance with their friends.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Bɛlbwa waa wola o pen teŋe la ta n nɛra nɛɛ jae kpa, dɛkalkɛŋɔ o ta n fɔo la ta teŋɛ.”
translated: The elephant and the rabbit went to the river to drink water, but the rabbit's calabash was broken by the elephant.

ref: But Laban told him, “If you really are my friend, stay on, and I'll pay whatever you ask. I'm sure the Lord has blessed me because of you.”
text: Gel n jemɛ kembɔr maa chɛ kɛ n kpɔ tɛɛ, ásɛ n nyin tɛɛ.”
translated: The person who has not gone to the farm, let him not say that the farm is far.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Hé berɛ ɡel ó jemɛ kɛŋɔ o bonɔɔ Jekɔb ɡe lɔ habɔɔ dɛɛ ba. Dɛ ń tɔ sie, dɛ́ kpɔ koŋkɛɛla nɔ ba tɛɛ, ásɛ mɔɔ o tel he be. Ásɛ dɛ he nao waa, he ken ɛɛ kpɔɔ kɛɔ.”
translated: They said that Jacob's well was very deep. When the time came, they would roll a big stone to cover it, and then they would go home. When they came back, they would roll the stone away again.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

text: Anomanta, Nowa, ka nyin daa waa kpeɡa, dɛ́ kwaa dakajen waa won nee sie vala kɛŋɔ ɡbera, ásɛ dɛ bɔne o bini kɛŋɔ deene, ásɛ dɛ kpɔ kootal maa o bini ne o habɔɔ bwa.
